ucs.title: [LLM] Strider / ProjectSauron LSA password-filter DLL registration (Notification Packages persistence)
ucs.description: Strider's signature credential-theft trick is registering a malicious password filter DLL via HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\Notification Packages so lsass.exe loads it on boot and feeds every plaintext password change to the implant. This UC alerts on any non-default DLL name appearing in Notification / Authentication / Security Packages and (where available) any non-system DLL loaded by lsass.exe — the pair is high-fidelity for Strider/ProjectSauron tradecraft documented by Symantec and Kaspersky.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where (Registry.registry_path="*\\Control\\Lsa\\*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Control\\LsaExtensionConfig\\*") AND Registry.registry_value_name IN ("Notification Packages","Authentication Packages","Security Packages") AND Registry.action IN ("modified","created","set") by host Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data Registry.user Registry.process_id Registry.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | eval pkg_list=split(lower(replace(registry_value_data,"\x00",",")),",") | mvexpand pkg_list | rex field=pkg_list "^(?<pkg>[a-z0-9_\-]+)" | where NOT match(pkg,"^(scecli|rassfm|kdcsvc|wdigest|msv1_0|negoexts|kerberos|cloudap|tspkg|pku2u|schannel|nego2|livessp)?$") | stats min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(pkg) as suspicious_pkg values(registry_value_data) as full_value values(process_name) as writer_process values(user) as writer_user by host registry_path registry_value_name | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let _default_lsa_packages = dynamic(["scecli","rassfm","kdcsvc","wdigest","msv1_0","negoexts","kerberos","cloudap","tspkg","pku2u","schannel","nego2","livessp",""]);
let _system_dirs = dynamic([@"c:\windows\system32\", @"c:\windows\syswow64\", @"c:\windows\winsxs\"]);
// 1. Notification / Authentication / Security Packages mutated to include a non-default DLL name
let RegPersistence = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Control\Lsa"
    | where RegistryValueName in~ ("Notification Packages","Authentication Packages","Security Packages")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("trustedinstaller.exe","msiexec.exe","poqexec.exe")
    | extend RawValue = tolower(replace_string(tostring(RegistryValueData), "\u0000", ","))
    | extend PackageList = split(RawValue, ",")
    | mv-expand Pkg = PackageList to typeof(string)
    | where isnotempty(Pkg)
    | where Pkg !in (_default_lsa_packages)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, DeviceId, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, SuspiciousPkg = Pkg,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountDomain, InitiatingProcessSHA256;
// 2. (Corroborator) lsass.exe loading a DLL outside system32/syswow64 — Strider's filter DLL writes itself to a custom path
let LsassLoad = DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "lsass.exe"
    | extend FolderLower = tolower(FolderPath)
    | where not(FolderLower has_any (_system_dirs))
    | project LoadTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, LoadedDll = FileName, LoadedFolder = FolderPath, LoadedSHA256 = SHA256;
RegPersistence
| join kind=leftouter LsassLoad on DeviceId
| where isnull(LoadTime) or LoadTime between (Timestamp - 1d .. Timestamp + 7d)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _default_lsa_packages = dynamic(["scecli","rassfm","kdcsvc","wdigest","msv1_0","negoexts","kerberos","cloudap","tspkg","pku2u","schannel","nego2","livessp",""]);
// Path A: ASIM registry telemetry (Sysmon EID 13 / EDR connectors)
let ASIMHits = ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Control\Lsa"
    | where RegistryValue in~ ("Notification Packages","Authentication Packages","Security Packages")
    | where ActingProcessName !endswith "trustedinstaller.exe"
    | extend RawValue = tolower(replace_string(tostring(RegistryValueData), "\u0000", ","))
    | mv-expand Pkg = split(RawValue, ",") to typeof(string)
    | where isnotempty(Pkg) and Pkg !in (_default_lsa_packages)
    | project TimeGenerated, Host=DvcHostname, RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData, SuspiciousPkg=Pkg,
              ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, ActorUsername, Source="ImRegistryEvent";
// Path B: Windows native registry-audit channel (EID 4657)
let SecHits = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4657
    | where ObjectName has @"\Control\Lsa"
    | where ObjectValueName in~ ("Notification Packages","Authentication Packages","Security Packages")
    | extend NewValue = tolower(extract(@"New Value:\s*([^<]+)", 1, EventData))
    | extend RawValue = replace_string(NewValue, "\u0000", ",")
    | mv-expand Pkg = split(RawValue, ",") to typeof(string)
    | where isnotempty(Pkg) and Pkg !in (_default_lsa_packages)
    | project TimeGenerated, Host=Computer, RegistryKey=ObjectName, RegistryValue=ObjectValueName, RegistryValueData=NewValue,
              SuspiciousPkg=Pkg, ActingProcessName=ProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine="", ActorUsername=SubjectUserName,
              Source="SecurityEvent4657";
union isfuzzy=true ASIMHits, SecHits
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Strider / ProjectSauron Password Filter DLL Persistence via LSA Notification Packages
id: 4d2c0e5e-c2d2-4d3a-9c2c-b8d6f5a1f0e2
status: experimental
description: Detects writes to the LSA Notification/Authentication/Security Packages registry values that introduce a DLL name outside the known Microsoft defaults. This is the signature persistence/credential-access mechanism reported for Strider (ProjectSauron, G0041) and registers a malicious password filter DLL with lsass.exe so plaintext password changes are exfiltrated.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0041/
  - https://securelist.com/faq-the-projectsauron-apt/75533/
  - https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/threat-intelligence/strider-cyberespionage
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.credential-access
  - attack.t1556.002
  - attack.t1547.002
  - attack.g0041
logsource:
  category: registry_set
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|contains:
      - '\Control\Lsa\Notification Packages'
      - '\Control\Lsa\Authentication Packages'
      - '\Control\Lsa\Security Packages'
  filter_default_only:
    Details|re: '^(?i)((scecli|rassfm|kdcsvc|wdigest|msv1_0|negoexts|kerberos|cloudap|tspkg|pku2u|schannel|nego2|livessp)\x00)+\x00?$'
  filter_servicing:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\poqexec.exe'
  condition: selection and not 1 of filter_*
falsepositives:
  - Approved enterprise password-complexity / DLP password filter DLLs deployed by GPO (rare; allowlist by DLL name + path + signer)
  - Specops, Anixis, nFront, Thycotic password filter products
level: high
ucs.rationale: Strider/ProjectSauron is one of the very few publicly tracked actors documented to install custom LSA password-filter DLLs as their primary credential-harvesting persistence — Kaspersky's ProjectSauron and Symantec's Strider write-ups both call this out as the implant's defining capability for harvesting domain-controller plaintext password changes. Most APTs steal credentials via lsass dumping (T
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Regular expression '^(?i)((scecli|rassfm|kdcsvc|wdigest|msv1_0|negoexts|kerberos|cloudap|tspkg|pku2u|schannel|nego2|livessp)\x00)+\x00?$' is invalid: global flags not at the start of the expression at position 1
